SeguroTools

Florida insurance rules and official resources

Official source guide for Florida insurance complaints, licensing checks, consumer alerts, and state-sensitive coverage questions.

Reviewed: 2026-06-05 · Source map, not legal or insurance advice

Official state source

Florida Department of Financial Services

Use the official department page for current complaint instructions, licensing lookup, company status, consumer alerts, and market notices. Static pages should not freeze changing state rules such as minimum limits, rate filings, or emergency bulletins.

Issues to review in this state

  • hurricane, flood, and homeowners claim records
  • agent and company verification
  • property insurance complaint preparation

Verification checklist

  • Open the state insurance department before relying on a quote or social answer.
  • Search license or company status before submitting sensitive data.
  • For claim disputes, save the denial letter, policy section, adjuster notes, photos, invoices, and all dates.
  • For health coverage, check whether the question belongs to a state DOI, HealthCare.gov, Medicare, Medicaid, or an employer plan administrator.

What can vary by state

Complaint forms, appeal deadlines, producer licensing, guaranty association details, auto requirements, homeowners availability, FAIR or residual market access, flood and disaster notices, and health-plan oversight can all differ. Treat a national article as the starting point and the state page as the controlling verification layer.

Documents to gather before contacting the department

  • Policy declarations page or benefit summary
  • Quote, renewal notice, cancellation notice, denial letter, or claim number
  • Timeline with dates, names, phone numbers, emails, photos, invoices, and repair estimates
  • Any Spanish translation or interpretation notes needed to compare English policy wording

Educational estimate only. This site does not sell insurance, negotiate coverage, or provide legal, tax, financial, or insurance advice.

State source shelf

  1. Florida Department of Financial Services (FL)
  2. NAIC State Insurance Departments (NAIC)
  3. NAIC Consumer Resources (NAIC)
  4. HealthCare.gov (CMS)
  5. Medicare.gov (CMS)
  6. FEMA Flood Insurance (FEMA)

Use the state department for current complaint, licensing, rate, and market notices.
